my_thoughts: The reversal works: memory = impartial supply (not source), supplies material for whatever you want. The student proposed memory as source (misattributing causality), which shows they understand: memory = source (causal link). They'll likely ask about mechanical status next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student proposed memory as source ('I notice that imagining seems to take place in memory, going from one association to another. Is this a correct observation?')—misattributing causality via offers empirical observation and proposes causal link. They understand: memory = source (causal link). The subject_object_inversion mechanism reverses: memory = impartial supply (not source), supplies material for whatever you want ('Memory by itself is not responsible for anything. Memory is impartial; it supplies material for whatever you want. You may want it for serious thinking, imagination, expression of negative emotions and so on'). Pattern: difficulty admission → self-calibrating method → memory question → reversal → [next: mechanical question]. They're grasping: memory = supply (not source), impartial (reversal).
visible_response:
No. Memory by itself is not responsible for anything. Memory is impartial; it
supplies material for whatever you want.
You may want it for serious thinking,
imagination, expression of negative emotions and so on.
